name: Nonlinear Least Squares (Levenberg-Marquardt)
applicable_scenarios: fit a nonlinear parametric model y = f(x; theta) to observations
calibration of physical / chemical rate constants
curve fitting when the response surface is smooth but non-linear
python_template:
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import least_squares

def nls_fit(f, theta0, x, y):
    # f(theta, x) -> predicted y of shape (n,).
    def residual(theta):
        return f(theta, x) - y
    result = least_squares(residual, theta0, method='lm')
    return result.x, result
typical_cases: logistic growth fit to epidemic curves
common_pitfalls: sensitive to initial guess; bad theta0 yields local minima
requires a differentiable (or at least smooth) residual function
ill-conditioned Jacobians need bounds or reparameterization
LM assumes Gaussian-like residuals; use robust loss for outliers
